inoculant_or_formulation: PGPR consortium of Pseudomonas fluorescens biotype G + Enterobacter cloacae ss disolvens + Serratia marcescens ss marcescens
key_result: Consortium inoculation outperformed individual strains; reported grain yield reached 2.7 t/ha with about 2.2x increase over control
notes: Good anchor case for consortium superiority under real crop conditions.

inoculant_or_formulation: ACC-deaminase PGPR with compost-based biofertilizer formulation
key_result: Stress-adapted strain CC7 performed better under rainfed conditions and compost-based formulation was more effective for yield improvement
notes: Useful for showing that field fit and formulation matter more than generic inoculation.

key_result: Biofilm logic is presented as a way to improve survival, colonization, nutrient uptake, and pathogen suppression compared with planktonic delivery
notes: Important for the delivery layer even when not yet a universal field benchmark.

crop_context: artificial grasslands in alpine mining region
inoculant_or_formulation: EM bacterial fertilizer plus organic fertilizer
key_result: Combined treatment promoted vegetation growth across two seasons while excessive dosage showed threshold-related inhibition
notes: Strong example that co-application can work well but dosage still matters.

crop_context: cotton under fresh brackish and saline irrigation
field_context: two-year field experiment under irrigation salinity
key_result: Biofertilizer addition increased lint cotton yield under fresh and brackish irrigation but benefit was less pronounced under stronger saline stress
notes: Important because it clearly marks both benefit and boundary conditions.

key_result: Microbial inoculants improved leaf morphology and physiology metrics and these changes were positively linked to yield, but field efficacy varied with management and environment
notes: Useful as a broad validation layer rather than a crop-specific recommendation.
